title: Keep TLS certificate and hostname verification strict
description: Verify the server certificate chain and hostname on every connection; never disable verification to silence an error.
body: Use when configuring any TLS client connection. Do not disable certificate verification, skip the hostname check, or trust all certs to get past a handshake error, because that opens the connection to man-in-the-middle interception. Actions: keep chain and hostname verification on, point the client at the correct CA bundle (or pin where appropriate), and fix the real cause of a handshake failure (expired cert, missing intermediate, wrong SNI, clock skew). Validation signals include a successful handshake against the real CA and a rejected connection to a server presenting a wrong/expired cert. Risk boundary: disabling TLS verification anywhere it touches real traffic is a security finding requiring a human gate; fix the certificate instead.
